summary: Verified both bug fixes for MediTrack Special Ordinations. Bug Fix 1: Special ordinations now correctly appear on Dashboard when the specified date arrives. Bug Fix 2: Time of day selection (Morgen, Middag, etc.) is now correctly highlighted/persisted when editing an ordination.

issues: HTML warning: <span> inside <option> element causes React hydration warning

passed_tests: REGISTRATION: Register new user with PIN 1234
ADD MEDICINE: Created Panodil 500mg with stock 30, reminder 7 days
SCHEDULE: Navigate to Schedule page and click 'Tilføj' button
SELECT MEDICINE: Select Panodil from dropdown
SPECIAL ORDINATION: Click Special Ordination button and popup opens
TIME OF DAY: Select 'Morgen 08:00' time slot
START DATE: Set start date to March 11, 2026 (today)
SAVE ORDINATION: Save the ordination configuration
SAVE SCHEDULE: Save the complete schedule entry
BUG FIX TEST 1 PASSED: Dashboard (Oversigt) shows Panodil with 'Special ordination' label for today (March 11)
BUG FIX TEST 2 PASSED: In edit mode, 'Morgen 08:00' button is HIGHLIGHTED (bg-emerald-500) showing selection persists

bugs_verified_fixed.description: Special ordinations don't show on Dashboard when the specified date arrives
bugs_verified_fixed.fix_details: Added isSpecialOrdinationActive() function that checks if special ordination should appear based on start_date, end_date, and repeat pattern (daily/weekly/biweekly/monthly)

bugs_verified_fixed.description: Time of day (Morgen, Middag, etc.) is not persisted when editing an ordination - buttons appear deactivated
bugs_verified_fixed.fix_details: Line 621 now correctly passes formData.slot_id || editingEntry?.slot_id as time_of_day_id when opening ordination popup for editing

bug_fix_1.verification: Dashboard shows 'Panodil 500mg' under 'Morgen 08:00' with 'Special ordination' label in green text

bug_fix_2.verification: Special ordination popup in edit mode shows 'Morgen 08:00' button highlighted with emerald-500 background color

critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard.js: isSpecialOrdinationActive() correctly handles daily/weekly/biweekly/monthly repeat patterns
Schedule.js: Edit mode properly passes time_of_day_id to ordination popup via ordData state
seed_data_creation: Created test user with Panodil medicine and special ordination starting March 11, 2026 with daily repeat
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both special ordination bugs have been verified as fixed. Bug 1: Dashboard now shows special ordinations on their active dates using isSpecialOrdinationActive() function. Bug 2: Editing an ordination correctly pre-selects the time of day (e.g., Morgen) in the popup. The special ordination feature is fully functional.

bug_1.root_cause: Dashboard was only checking day_doses for the selected day, not considering special_ordination entries
bug_1.fix: Added isSpecialOrdinationActive() function and updated todaySchedule logic to include entries where special_ordination is active

bug_2.root_cause: When opening ordination popup for editing, the time_of_day_id was not being passed correctly from the existing entry
bug_2.fix: Changed line 621 to use formData.slot_id || editingEntry?.slot_id ensuring the slot_id is always passed
